content: read reviews/95.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/95.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/95.md.

1→# Review: deepmind/95.lean
     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→
     5→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     7→
     8→## 2. Import Statement
     9→
    10→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` on line 17.
    11→- [x] No other imports are present.
    12→
    13→## 3. Module Docstring
    14→
    15→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–30).
    16→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 95`.
    17→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/95`.
    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (sum of squared distance multiplicities bound).
    19→- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[GuKa15]` Guth & Katz (2015).
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos95 ... end Erdos95`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos95`.
    25→- [x] `open Classical` appears **before** the namespace block (line 32).
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `distinctDistances` (line 39) has a docstring (lines 36–38).
    30→- [x] `distMultiplicity` (line 45) has a docstring (lines 42–44).
    31→- [x] Both definitions are marked `noncomputable` (appropriate since they use classical decidability on reals).
    32→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    33→- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `distinctDistances`, `distMultiplicity`.
    34→- N/A: No local notation needed; no custom types/Props defined.
    35→- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for the helper definitions. *Optional but recommended — consider adding tests verifying e.g. `distMultiplicity` on a small explicit point set.*
    36→
    37→## 6. Theorem Statements
    38→
    39→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 58).
    40→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_95` — matches namespace number, `snake_case`.
    41→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line (line 57): `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
    42→- [x] Has a docstring (lines 48–56) describing the bound and referencing Guth–Katz.
    43→- [x] Docstring references source paper: "Guth and Katz (2015)".
    44→
    45→### `answer()` usage
    46→
    47→The original problem asserts a bound: "the sum $\sum_i f(u_i)^2 \ll_\varepsilon n^{3+\varepsilon}$." This is a **direct assertion** (Case 3).
    48→
    49→- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly with no `answer()` wrapper — correct for Case 3.
    50→- [x] Problem is solved (Guth–Katz 2015), category is `research solved`, and the assertion is stated positively (not negated) — all consistent.
    51→
    52→## 7. Category Attribute
    53→
    54→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    55→- [x] Value is `research solved` — valid; the problem was proved by Guth & Katz (2015) but not formally verified in Lean.
    56→- N/A: `research open` sorry-rule does not apply here.
    57→
    58→## 8. AMS Attribute
    59→
    60→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 52`.
    61→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 52).
    62→- [x] Numbers in ascending order: 5 < 52.
    63→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    64→- [x] Both codes are valid MSC2020 codes: **5** (Combinatorics), **52** (Convex and discrete geometry). Both are appropriate for a combinatorial geometry distance problem.
    65→
    66→## 9. Proof Bodies
    67→
    68→- [x] Uses `by sorry` pattern. The proof is split as `:= by` / `sorry` (lines 64–65) due to the multi-line type signature — this matches the acceptable multi-line pattern from the checklist.
    69→- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
    70→- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
    71→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    72→
    73→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    74→
    75→- N/A: No variants present. The file formalizes a single conjecture.
    76→
    77→## 11. Code Quality
    78→
    79→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    80→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `≤`, `≠`.
    81→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    82→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    83→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    84→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    85→
    86→## 12. Build Verification
    87→
    88→- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review. No obvious issues; the only expected warning is `sorry` in the proof of `erdos_95`.
    89→- [ ] Custom linter compliance not verified by build, but manual inspection shows conformance with all five linters:
    90→  - `linter.style.copyright` — correct header.
    91→  - `linter.style.category_attribute` — single valid category.
    92→  - `linter.style.ams_attribute` — single valid AMS tag, ordered, no duplicates.
    93→  - `linter.style.answer_attribute` — no `answer()` used (correct for Case 3).
    94→  - `linter.style.namespace` — all declarations inside `Erdos95`.
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→**Verdict:** Pass. All mandatory checklist items are satisfied. The only optional suggestion is adding `@[category test]` sanity-check examples for the helper definitions `distinctDistances` and `distMultiplicity`.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/95.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` on line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–30).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 95`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/95`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (sum of squared distance multiplicities bound).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[GuKa15]` Guth & Katz (2015).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos95 ... end Erdos95`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos95`.
- [x] `open Classical` appears **before** the namespace block (line 32).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `distinctDistances` (line 39) has a docstring (lines 36–38).
- [x] `distMultiplicity` (line 45) has a docstring (lines 42–44).
- [x] Both definitions are marked `noncomputable` (appropriate since they use classical decidability on reals).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `distinctDistances`, `distMultiplicity`.
- N/A: No local notation needed; no custom types/Props defined.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for the helper definitions. *Optional but recommended — consider adding tests verifying e.g. `distMultiplicity` on a small explicit point set.*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 58).
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_95` — matches namespace number, `snake_case`.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line (line 57): `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (lines 48–56) describing the bound and referencing Guth–Katz.
- [x] Docstring references source paper: "Guth and Katz (2015)".

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asserts a bound: "the sum $\sum_i f(u_i)^2 \ll_\varepsilon n^{3+\varepsilon}$." This is a **direct assertion** (Case 3).

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly with no `answer()` wrapper — correct for Case 3.
- [x] Problem is solved (Guth–Katz 2015), category is `research solved`, and the assertion is stated positively (not negated) — all consistent.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` — valid; the problem was proved by Guth & Katz (2015) but not formally verified in Lean.
- N/A: `research open` sorry-rule does not apply here.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 52`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 52).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order: 5 < 52.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both codes are valid MSC2020 codes: **5** (Combinatorics), **52** (Convex and discrete geometry). Both are appropriate for a combinatorial geometry distance problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by sorry` pattern. The proof is split as `:= by` / `sorry` (lines 64–65) due to the multi-line type signature — this matches the acceptable multi-line pattern from the checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The file formalizes a single conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `≤`, `≠`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review. No obvious issues; the only expected warning is `sorry` in the proof of `erdos_95`.
- [ ] Custom linter compliance not verified by build, but manual inspection shows conformance with all five linters:
  - `linter.style.copyright` — correct header.
  - `linter.style.category_attribute` — single valid category.
  - `linter.style.ams_attribute` — single valid AMS tag, ordered, no duplicates.
  - `linter.style.answer_attribute` — no `answer()` used (correct for Case 3).
  - `linter.style.namespace` — all declarations inside `Erdos95`.

---

**Verdict:** Pass. All mandatory checklist items are satisfied. The only optional suggestion is adding `@[category test]` sanity-check examples for the helper definitions `distinctDistances` and `distMultiplicity`.

content.text: The review passed with verdict "**Pass**". No changes needed to `deepmind/95.lean`.
